  \caption{Characteristics of the Trained Forest}
  \label{fig:forest_characteristics}
  \centering

  % Panel 1: Tree depth distribution (table)
  \begin{minipage}[t]{0.34\textwidth}
    \centering
    \subcaption{Tree depth distribution}\label{fig:tree_depth}
    \vspace{0.1em}
    \input{output/tables/tree_depth}
  \end{minipage}
  \hfill
  % Panel 2: Organizational practice split frequency (generated by fold_comparisons.R)
  \begin{minipage}[t]{0.58\textwidth}
    \centering
    \subcaption{Organizational practice split frequency}\label{fig:var_imp}
    \vspace{0.1em}
    \resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{\input{\AnalyzeForestDir/variable_importance.tex}}
  \end{minipage}

    \par 
      \vspace{0.5em}
  \centering
  \begin{minipage}[b]{0.7\textwidth}
    \centering
    \subcaption{Organizational practice score cross-correlations}\label{fig:practice_corr}
    \vspace{0.1em}
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{\AnalyzeForestDir/pc_score_correlation_heatmap.png}
  \end{minipage}


  % Figure notes
  \begin{minipage}{1\textwidth}
    \vspace{0.75em}
    \textbf{Figure notes:}
    Panel~\subref{fig:tree_depth} reports the distribution of tree depth across the trees of the binarized practice-score forests, pooled with equal weight per forest across the forests for organizations with exactly one and exactly two key members.
    Panel~\subref{fig:var_imp} reports the frequency with which each organizational practice score is used to split the sample, averaged with equal weight across the cross-fitting fold forests for organizations with exactly one and exactly two key members, and weighted by tree depth following the variable-importance measure in GRF 2.4.0.
    Panel~\subref{fig:practice_corr} depicts a heatmap of correlations across all organizational practice scores, pooled across organizations with exactly one and exactly two key members.
  \end{minipage}
\end{figure}
